mnemonics.title: WINA Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: WINA = Write In, No Allocate - Remember: When you Write IN A miss, No memory comes IN (to cache). Write Allocate: When you Write and Allocate, memory is fetched INTO cache.

mnemonics.title: WWWB Combo
mnemonics.content: Write-back + Write-allocate = Wasted Writes Well Allocated (delays writing)

mnemonics.title: WTNWA Pattern
mnemonics.content: Write-Through = No-Write Allocate - Think: Through means going straight to memory, bypassing cache allocation

acronyms.usage: Policy that fetches block into cache on write miss
Policy that writes directly to memory on write miss, bypassing cache
Alternative name for Write Allocate policy
Eviction policy often used with write allocate to determine which line to replace

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: Write Miss → Check Policy → If WA → Fetch Block to Cache → Write to Cache → Set Dirty Bit (if write-back)
Draw a flowchart: Write Miss → Check Policy → If NWA → Write Directly to Memory → Done (no cache update)
Use a state diagram showing: Valid + Clean, Valid + Dirty, Invalid states with transitions on read/write hits/misses

analogies.analogy: Like going to a library to borrow a book (cache) before writing notes in it, even if you only need to write one note - in case you need to read more later.
Like writing a letter directly in the mailbox (main memory) without taking it home (cache) first - useful if you're only mailing it once.
Like working on a rough draft kept at your desk (cache) - you modify it many times, and only copy to the permanent file (main memory) when you're done or need the desk space.
